sections.paragraph: a young he-camel, already fit for carrying light burdens (comp. עַיִר and עֵגֶל). Pl. const. בִּכְרֵי, Isa 60:6. To this answers the Arab. بَكْرُ a young camel, which they observe signifies the same age as الفتى a young man, in men; see Bochart, Hieroz. i. p. 82, seq. See also my remarks in Comment. on Isa. loc. cit. and in Thes. page 236. [“Comp. Root בָּכַר No. 3.”]
